zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Susan de Clifford於1500年出生在英國肯特波炳時，她的父親Nicholas Clifford48歲，母親Mildred Bourne45歲。她於1525年與Sir Edward Moore在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1613年6月7日在美國密西根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦享年113歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Susan de Clifford于1500年出生在英国肯特波炳时，她的父亲Nicholas Clifford48岁，母亲Mildred Bourne45岁。她于1525年与Sir Edward Moore在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1613年6月7日在美国密歇根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦享年113岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦聖 登斯坦 在 馮 韋斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Susan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Suzan
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Susanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Susan de Clifford was born in 1500, in Bobbing, Kent, England, her father, Nicholas Clifford, was 48 and her mother, Mildred Bourne, was 45. She married Sir Edward Moore in 1525, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 7 June 1613, in London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, United States, at the age of 113, and was buried in St Dunstan in the West, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Susan de Clifford est née en 1500, à Bobbing, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Clifford, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Mildred Bourne, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward Moore en 1525, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 7 juin 1613, à London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, États-Unis, à l’âge de 113 ans, et a été enterrée à St Dunstan in the West, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.St. Dunstan-In-The-West, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 던스탠 인 테 웨스트

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Susan de Clifford(이)가 1500년 영국 켄트 보브빙에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Clifford(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mildred Bourne(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1525년 영국에서 Sir Edward Moore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1613년 6월 7일 미국 미시간 주 먼로 런던 타운시프 런던에서 113세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 던스탠 인 테 웨스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Susan de Clifford nasceu em 1500, em Bobbing, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Clifford, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Mildred Bourne, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edward Moore em 1525, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 7 de junho de 1613, em London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos da América, com 113 anos, e foi sepultada em St Dunstan in the West, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Susan de Clifford родилась в 1500 г. в местности боббинг, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Clifford было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Mildred Bourne, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward Moore в 1525 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 7 июня 1613 г. в местности лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 113 лет и была похоронена в местности ст дунстань ин т вест, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Susan de Clifford nació en 1500, en Bobbing, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Clifford, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Mildred Bourne, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Sir Edward Moore en 1525, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 7 de junio de 1613, en London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 113 años, y fue sepultada en St Dunstan in the West, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
